name: Cccs Of Sheboygan
description_short: NFCC-approved non-profit credit counseling organization serving Wisconsin and Minnesota with free financial assessments, debt management, and homeowner education.
description_long:
CCCS of Sheboygan is a non-profit credit counseling agency approved by the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC) operating across Wisconsin and Minnesota. The organization has established itself as a trusted financial advocate for consumers struggling with debt, credit issues, and overall financial management. Their service areas include Sheboygan, LaCrosse, West Bend, and Fond du Lac, with centralized intake and counseling operations.

The organization offers comprehensive financial counseling through certified counselors, including one-hour free consultations that cover financial assessments, credit score analysis, goal planning, and customized action plans. They provide debt management guidance, homeowner education and housing counseling, financial literacy courses led through partner organizations, and ongoing support for clients working toward financial stability. Their approach emphasizes education and personalized strategy rather than one-size-fits-all solutions.

CCCS distinguishes itself through NFCC accreditation, certified counselors, and a documented track record of client success—their website reports that the majority of clients increase savings and decrease debt by 10% within six months. They emphasize a non-judgmental approach to financial education, with client testimonials highlighting that their counseling "didn't make me feel stupid." The organization operates on a free or reduced-cost model, making professional financial guidance accessible to consumers across economic backgrounds.

The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are restricted to specific Wisconsin and Minnesota regions. While the organization clearly helps many clients, their website provides limited data on success rates beyond the stated 10% improvement figure, and specific pricing for non-free services is not disclosed online. As a counseling organization rather than a direct debt solution provider, they guide rather than directly resolve financial problems.
pros: NFCC-approved and staff includes certified financial counselors
Completely free one-hour initial consultation covering financial assessment, credit analysis, and action planning
Documented results: majority of clients achieve 10% debt reduction and savings increase within six months
Non-judgmental approach with client testimonials praising the educational quality and supportive environment
Comprehensive services including debt counseling, homeowner education, and financial literacy courses
Ongoing support available after initial counseling session
Multi-service approach addressing various financial challenges (debt, housing decisions, credit building)
cons: Geographic limitations: services only available in specific Wisconsin and Minnesota locations (Sheboygan, LaCrosse, West Bend, Fond du Lac areas)
Pricing for services beyond free consultation is not disclosed on website, creating uncertainty about affordability for ongoing counseling
As a counseling organization, they guide and educate rather than directly resolve debt or negotiate with creditors
Limited quantified success metrics beyond the 10% improvement claim; individual results may vary significantly
Website does not specify counselor credentials, response times, or appointment availability
best_for: Wisconsin and Minnesota residents struggling with debt and seeking free professional financial counseling
First-time credit counseling clients who need education on budgeting, credit scores, and debt management basics
Homeowners considering mortgages or facing housing decisions who benefit from homeowner education programs
Consumers without immediate access to paid financial advisors seeking comprehensive financial assessment
diagnosis: CCCS of Sheboygan is best for Wisconsin and Minnesota residents seeking free, professional, judgment-free financial counseling and education from NFCC-certified counselors. The main caveat is geographic restriction to their service areas and the counseling-based model, which means they educate and guide rather than directly negotiate debts or provide loans.
services: Free one-hour financial consultation and assessment
One-on-one credit counseling with certified counselors
Credit score review and credit report analysis
Financial literacy and educational courses (through partner organizations)
Ongoing counselor support after initial consultation
Goal planning and financial goal tracking
Budget development and money management guidance
Special programs (specific programs listed on website but not detailed in provided content)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
